name: ip
description: Show and manipulate routing, network devices, interfaces and tunnels

subcommands.name: address
subcommands.description: Protocol (IP or IPv6) address on a device

subcommands.name: addrlabel
subcommands.description: Label configuration for protocol address selection

subcommands.name: help
subcommands.description: Display a list of supported commands and arguments

subcommands.name: mrule
subcommands.description: Rule in multicast routing policy database

subcommands.name: vrf
subcommands.description: Manage virtual routing and forwarding devices

name: -V
-Version
options.description: Display the version of the ip utility

name: -s
-statistics
-stats
options.description: Output more information (may be used twice for even more)

name: -d
-details
options.description: Output more detailed information

name: -r
-resolve
options.description: Print DNS names instead of host addresses

name: -h
-human-readable
-human
options.description: Output statistics with human-readable values

options.name: -iec
options.description: Print human-readable rates in IEC units (e.g. 1Ki = 1024)

name: -j
-json
options.description: Output results in JSON format

name: -p
-pretty
options.description: Pretty-print JSON output (implies -json)

name: -f
-family
options.description: Protocol family to use
Shortcut for -family inet
Shortcut for -family inet6

options.name: -M
options.description: Shortcut for -family mpls

options.name: -B
options.description: Shortcut for -family bridge
Shortcut for -family link

name: -l
-loops
options.description: Maximum number of loops for 'ip address flush'

name: -br
-brief
options.description: Print only basic information in a tabular format

name: -o
-oneline
options.description: Output each record on a single line, replacing line feeds with backslashes

name: -t
-timestamp
options.description: Display current time when using monitor

name: -ts
-tshort
options.description: Like -timestamp, but use shorter format

name: -b
-batch
options.description: Read commands from provided file or standard input

name: -rc
-rcvbuf
options.description: Set the netlink socket receive buffer size (default: 1MB)

name: -n
-netns
options.description: Switch to the specified network namespace

name: -N
-Numeric
options.description: Print protocol, scope, dsfield, etc. as numbers instead of names

name: -a
-all
options.description: Execute specified command over all objects

name: -c
-color
options.description: Use color output

options.name: -force
options.description: Don't terminate on errors in batch mode
